name: monday-automation
description: Automate Monday.com work management including boards, items, columns, groups, subitems, and updates via Rube MCP (Composio). Always search tools first for current schemas.
capabilities: Create and manage Monday.com boards with custom columns and groups
Add, update, and organize items across different board structures
Query boards, groups, columns, and items with ID resolution
Update column values including status, dates, people, and timeline fields
Work with subitems and add comments or updates to items
Execute GraphQL mutations for advanced Monday.com operations
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis detected 191 'external_commands' patterns in SKILL.md, but these are all backtick-enclosed tool names (e.g., `MONDAY_LIST_BOARDS`, `RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS`) used as markdown code references in documentation. No actual code execution or shell commands present. Network reference is a legitimate HTTPS URL for Rube MCP server endpoint. This is documentation-only content with no executable code.
valueStatement: Stop manually managing Monday.com boards and items. This skill automates board creation, item updates, and team workflows through direct API integration.
userTitle: Automate Monday.com workflows with Rube MCP
limitations: Requires Rube MCP server connection and active Monday.com OAuth authentication
Cannot create subitems directly via MONDAY_CREATE_ITEM; must use GraphQL mutations
Column IDs must be resolved from tool responses before setting values
Different column types require specific JSON formats for value updates

useCases.targetUser: Project manager setting up team workspaces
useCases.title: Project Board Setup
useCases.description: Project managers create new Monday.com boards with predefined columns, groups, and status workflows for new initiatives

useCases.title: Task Status Automation
useCases.description: Team leads bulk update item statuses, assign team members, and set due dates across multiple Monday.com items

useCases.title: Workflow Integration
useCases.description: Developers integrate Monday.com with external systems by creating items, updating columns, and adding comments based on triggers

promptTemplates.title: Create a New Project Board
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Monday.com board called 'Q1 Marketing Campaign' in my default workspace with columns for Status, Due Date, and Assignee.
promptTemplates.scenario: Setting up a new project tracking board from scratch

promptTemplates.title: Update Multiple Item Statuses
promptTemplates.prompt: Find all items in the 'Development' group on board 'Product Roadmap' and update their status to 'Done'.
promptTemplates.scenario: Bulk updating task statuses after completion

promptTemplates.title: Add Items with Column Values
promptTemplates.prompt: Add 5 new items to board 'Content Calendar' in group 'February' with title 'Blog Post 1' through 'Blog Post 5', status 'To Do', and assigned to user ID 12345.
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating multiple tasks with predefined attributes

promptTemplates.title: Query and Reorganize Items
promptTemplates.prompt: List all items from board 'Backlog' that have status 'Blocked', then move them to the 'Priority Review' group and add an update comment requesting assessment.
promptTemplates.scenario: Finding and reorganizing blocked work items

bestPractices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current schemas before using Monday.com tools
Resolve display names to IDs before operations—use MONDAY_LIST_BOARDS to find board_id, MONDAY_LIST_COLUMNS for column_id
Use MONDAY_CHANGE_SIMPLE_COLUMN_VALUE for text/status/dropdown updates and MONDAY_UPDATE_ITEM for complex types like timeline or people
Test workflows on a test board first to verify column value formats before applying to production boards
antiPatterns: Do not use column titles (e.g., 'Status') directly—always resolve to column IDs (e.g., 'status_1') from tool responses
Do not mix up ID formats—board and item IDs are integers, but group IDs and column IDs are strings
Do not use MONDAY_CREATE_ITEM for subitems—subitems require GraphQL mutations through MONDAY_CREATE_OBJECT
Do not skip connection verification—always confirm Monday.com connection shows ACTIVE before running workflows

faq.question: How do I get started with this skill?
faq.answer: Add Rube MCP server (https://rube.app/mcp) to your client configuration, authenticate with Monday.com via RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS, then verify the connection shows ACTIVE status before use.

faq.question: Why does my column update fail with 'column not found'?
faq.answer: You are using the column title instead of the column ID. Call MONDAY_LIST_COLUMNS to get the actual column ID (e.g., 'status_1', 'date4') and use that in updates.

faq.question: Can I create subitems with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, but not through MONDAY_CREATE_ITEM. Use MONDAY_CREATE_OBJECT with a GraphQL 'create_subitem' mutation to create subitems on parent items.

faq.question: What is the difference between MONDAY_CHANGE_SIMPLE_COLUMN_VALUE and MONDAY_UPDATE_ITEM?
faq.answer: Use MONDAY_CHANGE_SIMPLE_COLUMN_VALUE for simple string values like status labels or text. Use MONDAY_UPDATE_ITEM for complex column types that require JSON objects like timeline, people, or date ranges.

faq.question: How do I find my board ID if I only know the board name?
faq.answer: Call MONDAY_LIST_BOARDS to see all boards, then match the board name to get its numeric board ID. Use this ID for all subsequent operations on that board.

faq.question: Does this skill work with private boards?
faq.answer: Yes, as long as your authenticated Monday.com account has access to the private board. The skill operates with your account's permissions and can access any board you can view.
